channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Putin says US push for Greenland rooted in history, vows to uphold Russian interest in the Arctic
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that President Donald Trump's push for control over Greenland wasn't surprising given longtime U.S. interest in the mineral-rich territory.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: King Charles admitted to hospital over side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: The United Kingdom’s King Charles III was admitted to the hospital Thursday due to unexpected side effects from cancer treatments.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Greenland lawmakers form a new government on the eve of Vance visit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Greenlandic lawmakers on Thursday agreed to form a new government, banding together to resist U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to annex the Arctic island, local media reported.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia to hold general elections on May 3 with inflation and a housing shortage major issues
item.description: Australians will go to the polls on May 3 with high costs of living and a shortage of housing key issues likely weighing against the government.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Danish PM says Greenlanders ‘have not been cowed’ ahead of Vance visit
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen praised Greenlanders and said they “have not been cowed” ahead of a Friday visit by Vice President Vance and other Trump administration officials to Greenland. Greenland is an autonomous territory that is part of the Kingdom of Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: French president says not all European allies agree on a proposed force for Ukraine
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France and Britain will continue to forge ahead with plans to deploy troops in Ukraine to defend an eventual peace deal with Russia but only some other nations want to take part, French President Emmanuel Macron said Thursday after a summit of countries that have been mulling the propos
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, SAMUEL PETREQUIN and BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rubio warns visas will be revoked for all foreign student ‘activists’ amid Tufts arrest
item.description: Rubio says student visas are for studying, “not to become a social activist," and warns they’ll be revoked if foreigners are seen as engaging in “destabilizing" acts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Sudan opposition says peace deal has collapsed after arrest of its leader
item.description: JUBA, South Sudan (AP) — South Sudan’s main opposition party said Thursday a peace agreement that ended a five-year civil war has collapsed, following the arrest of its leader Riek Machar a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: DENG MACHOL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stefanik nomination for UN ambassador faces fresh uncertainty
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) nomination to serve as ambassador to the United Nations is facing fresh uncertainty as the Trump administration weighs the risks of further whittling down the Republican majority in the House.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Rubio vows stern response if Venezuela attacks Guyana
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has vowed that the United States will respond forcefully if Venezuela attacks Guyana amid an ongoing territorial dispute that involves massive oil and gas reserves.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Europe says no to Trump push to lift sanctions on Russia, upending Black Sea ceasefire
item.description: Europe says no to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure some sanction relief for Russia as he tries to make a Black Sea ceasefire deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 dead after tourist submarine sinks off Egypt's coast: officials
item.description: Six dead, dozens rescued after tourist submarine sinks off the coast on Egypt on Thursday. Officials say all of the victims were Russian. It is not known what caused the sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea sent 3,000 more troops to Russia, according to South's assessment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea sent around 3,000 additional troops to Russia in January and February in continued support for Russian President Vladimir Putin’s war on Ukraine, South Korea’s military said Thursday in its latest assessment.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill a family of 6 and a Hamas spokesman in Gaza
item.description: Separate Israeli strikes have killed a family of six and a Hamas spokesman in the northern Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Crowds flock to newest Catholic saint in Assisi
item.description: ASSISI, Italy (AP) — Pilgrims have been pouring into this medieval hilltop town to venerate not only two of the Catholic Church’s most celebrated saints, Francis and Clare, but its newest — Carlo Acutis, the first millennial saint, who will be canonized on April 27. “St. Francis, St.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police use force to break up protests at a university in Turkey's capital
item.description: Police have used pepper spray, plastic pellets and water cannon against protesters in Turkey’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Robert Badendieck, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brazilian Supreme Court panel orders ex-President Bolsonaro to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A panel of Brazil Supreme Court justices on Wednesday unanimously accepted charges against former President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, and ordered him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea’s centuries-old Gounsa temple is left in ruins by unprecedented wildfires
item.description: Wildfires in South Korea's southern regions have left 24 people dead and destroyed more than 300 structures, including large parts of an ancient Buddhist temple complex.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian ex-President Bolsonaro ordered to stand trial over alleged coup plan
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court accepted charges against ex-President Jair Bolsonaro over an alleged attempt to stay in office after his 2022 election defeat, ordering him to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search 'ongoing' for 4 American soldiers missing from training area in Lithuania: NATO
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing from a training ground in Lithuania during scheduled tactical training, NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Captured Ukrainians convicted on terrorism charges by Russia in what Kyiv called a sham trial
item.description: Russia on Wednesday convicted 23 captured Ukrainians on terrorism charges stemming from the war in Ukraine in a military court trial that Kyiv denounced as a sham and a violation of international law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-Hamas protests break out in Gaza Strip over demands to end war
item.description: Palestinians protested in northern Gaza, where they reportedly demanded an end to the war in anti-Hamas protests. The last major protest against the terror group occurred in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US goes international for eggs amid bird flu outbreak
item.description: Imported eggs, typically used for pet food, are now being used in processed foods like cake mixes and salad dressings.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill scores in Gaza as Egypt offers new ceasefire proposal
item.description: Israeli strikes across the Gaza Strip killed more than 65 Palestinians over the past day, including women and children, the Gaza Health Ministry said Monday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis will be released from the hospital on Sunday, after 38 days battling a life-threatening case of pneumonia in both lungs, his doctors said.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Heathrow says some flights will resume after a fire cut power to Europe's busiest airport
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heathrow Airport said it planned to resume some flights Friday after a large fire at an electrical substation knocked out power to Europe’s busiest flight hub and disrupted global travel for hundreds of thousands of passengers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, DANICA KIRKA and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel returns to war in Gaza with wider aims and almost no constraints
item.description: Israel's renewed military offensive in the Gaza Strip threatens to be even deadlier and more destructive than the last, as it pursues wider aims with far fewer constraints.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9
